content.verdict: The Lodge at Kauri Cliffs sits on 6,000 acres of Northland farmland with a clifftop championship golf course rated among the world's finest, two private beaches at the foot of the cliffs, and a main lodge of 22 suites and cottages framing the Pacific Ocean. Owned by the Robertson family alongside Cape Kidnappers and Matakauri, Kauri Cliffs is the genteel honeymoon choice — sub-tropical garden walks, world-class golf, and a polished service ethic that has earned it consistent inclusion among the world's top lodges.
content.best_room: The Deluxe Suites in the cottage clusters across the gardens have private terraces, fireplaces, and ocean or fairway views. The Owner's Cottage is the most expensive option with three bedrooms, private pool, and dedicated chef. For honeymooners, an ocean-facing Deluxe Suite delivers both privacy and the property's signature Pacific view from the bed.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Clifftop Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 3.5 hours from Auckland through the Northland countryside or take the scenic helicopter transfer (45 minutes) to the lodge's private pad. Pre-dinner drinks on the main lodge terrace looking across to Cape Brett and the Cavalli Islands. Dinner in the formal dining room with Northland produce and an extensive New Zealand wine list.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Kauri Cliffs Golf
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Kauri Cliffs course, designed by David Harman, is rated by Golf Digest among the world's top 100 — six holes play directly along the Pacific cliffs with the open ocean to one side. A round for golfers, or a tour and lesson for non-golfers. Afternoon at the spa or by the heated infinity pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Bay of Islands Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive or fly to nearby Bay of Islands for the historic Waitangi Treaty Grounds, then a sailing charter through the 144 islands. Hole in the Rock at Cape Brett, dolphin encounters, and lunch on the water. Return to Kauri Cliffs for spa treatments and dinner on the terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private Beach & Coastal Walks
itinerary_7_nights.description: Kauri Cliffs has two private beaches accessible only by 4WD shuttle from the lodge — Pink Beach and a secondary beach below the cliffs. Spend the day with a lodge picnic, swimming in the warm Northland water, and walking the coastal cliff trails through pohutukawa and native bush.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cape Reinga & Ninety Mile Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: A long but unforgettable drive or helicopter trip to Cape Reinga — the spiritual northernmost point of New Zealand where the Tasman Sea and Pacific Ocean meet. Ninety Mile Beach drive, sand-dune slides at Te Paki, and traditional fish-and-chips at a Northland coastal town.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Maori Cultural Experience
itinerary_7_nights.description: Northland is the cradle of Maori arrival in New Zealand — visit the Waitangi Treaty Grounds for a guided cultural tour, traditional waka (canoe) ride, and an evening hangi feast and haka performance. Return to the lodge for the final night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Round or Spa & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final round at Kauri Cliffs or a long spa morning before the transfer back to Auckland for the international connection. Many honeymooners pair Kauri Cliffs with Cape Kidnappers (also Robertson Lodges) for a complete North Island lodge tour, or continue to the South Island.

caveats: Kauri Cliffs is golf-centric — the property's culture and a meaningful share of guests are there for the course; non-golfers are welcomed but should embrace the country-club character.
The two beaches are 10-15 minute 4WD trips from the lodge — there is no walk-in beach access from the rooms; couples wanting beach-front accommodation should consider Helena Bay instead.
Northland's weather is changeable — sub-tropical summer storms are common; check the forecast and build flexibility into Cape Reinga or sailing day plans.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Kauri Cliffs is a strong honeymoon choice for couples who appreciate refined country-house hospitality, world-class golf, and the dramatic Northland coastline. The clifftop setting, private beaches, and the polished Robertson Lodges service standard create a romantic and unhurried atmosphere.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: November through April is the warm Northland season — ideal for golf, beach days, and water activities. December-February is summer peak. May-September is cooler with green countryside, log fires, and lower rates; the golf course remains playable year-round.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Kauri Cliffs books out 4-6 months ahead for peak summer and major golf weeks. The Owner's Cottage often books a year ahead. The lodge accepts approximately 50 guests across all suites and cottages.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Kauri Cliffs is not adults-only — children are welcomed in the cottage suites and Owner's Cottage. The main lodge atmosphere skews adult and the property's golf and fine-dining focus naturally attracts couples and golf groups.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: An ocean-facing Deluxe Suite is the romantic honeymoon choice — private terrace, fireplace, and the Pacific view that defines the property. The cottage layout means each suite feels detached and private.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Auckland International Airport is the gateway — drive 3.5 hours through Northland or take the 45-minute helicopter transfer for the scenic arrival. The lodge arranges all logistics; the closest small airport is Kerikeri (40 minutes) for connections from Auckland.
